title: Slow-cooker braised pork belly with cider
subtitle: Pork belly, dry cider, apple, and thyme
excerpt: A whole slab of pork belly braised in dry cider with eating apple, garlic, and thyme for eight hours until the fat has softened completely and the meat yields with no resistance. The cider reduces to a lightly sweet, savoury braising liquid. Serve with mashed potato.
sourceNotes: Braised pork belly is a technique shared across many European cuisines. The British version with dry cider and apple follows the same Norman logic as Normandy-style pork, the acid in the cider tenderises the meat, the apple provides sweetness, and the long braise renders the fat until it becomes yielding rather than unctuous. The slow cooker is the ideal vessel: it maintains the low, steady temperature that the method requires without any attention.

content.text: Pork belly contains two distinct layers, the lean meat below and the fat on top. The goal is to cook it long enough that the fat becomes translucent and yielding rather than greasy. At six hours it still has some resistance; at eight it collapses under a spoon.

Season the {{pork-belly}} all over with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}. Scatter the {{onion}}, {{apple-eating}}, and {{garlic}} in the base of the slow cooker. Set the pork on top, skin-side up. Mix the {{dijon-mustard}} into the {{dry-cider}} and pour around the pork along with the {{stock-chicken}}. Tuck in the {{thyme-fresh}} and {{bay-leaves}}. Cook on low for 8 hours, until the fat layer is soft and yielding and the meat pulls easily. Transfer to a board and rest for 10 minutes. Strain the braising liquid into a saucepan and reduce by half over high heat for a sauce.

items.fix: Continue for another hour. Scoring the skin deeply before cooking also helps the fat render. The skin in a slow cooker will not crisp, for crackling, transfer to a hot grill for 5 minutes at the end
items.cause: Eight hours is not enough for some thicker belly cuts
items.symptom: Pork belly skin still tough and chewy

items.fix: Add a splash of cider vinegar and taste. The mustard also adds balance, stir in another teaspoon before reducing
items.cause: The apple has dissolved and sweetened the sauce significantly

items.fix: For a leaner belly, add 100 ml more stock. The meat should be at least half-submerged in the braising liquid
items.cause: The slab was very lean, or it sat above the liquid rather than in it

content.text: Braised pork belly is a weekend dinner. It requires no attention after the first ten minutes but rewards patience with eight hours of cooking. Slice it thickly and serve over mashed potato with the reduced cider sauce poured over. The braised apple pieces alongside are worth saving and serving as a soft, savoury accompaniment.
